summary: Clear soundboard capture of a rain‑soaked Eugene show with a monster Saint, magical Rain>Eyes sequence and soaring Wheel/Attics jams.
review: The recording is generally clear and well‑balanced – many reviewers call it one of the best they’ve heard, despite a slightly muddy start to the first set.  Musically the night is unforgettable: a thunderous “Saint of Circumstance,” an inspired Rain > Eyes combo, sparkling Samba in the Rain, and a seamless Wheel → Attics stretch make the second set legendary.  The vibe of the rain‑filled campground shines through, turning this into one of the most beloved 1994 Eugene performances.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 20 reviews overwhelmingly praise the performance (standout songs, band energy) and note good to excellent sound quality; weighting yields a high overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp and well balanced; several listeners say it sounds among the best they've heard, with only minor muddiness at the very start of the first set.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert started a bit slow, but the rain‑filled atmosphere and extended jams built momentum, culminating in a magical second set highlighted by Rain>Eyes, Saint→Drumz→Wheel→Attics.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Crunchy, powerful guitar tone especially on “Rain”; a few moments later in the show he sounds a bit off but overall strong.
band_member_comments.Phil: Charismatic banter about being a ‘democratic band’, sustained bass notes during jams, and a memorable kiss to the clouds; solid bass throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocal delivery on the Beatles’ “Rain” praised as one of the best Dead covers.
band_member_comments.Keys: Sparkling piano on “Samba in the Rain”, occasional MIDI effects noted, overall bright keyboard work.
band_member_comments.Drums: Powerful drum‑z section leading into space and Wheel; highlighted as a high‑energy performance.
